headline: GOLD EDGES TO $4,829: Safe-Haven Buying Resumes on Iran Peace Talk Hopes
snippet: Spot gold climbed to $4,829/oz on Thursday, up 0.63%, as diplomats in Washington and Tehran prepared a second round of talks ahead of the ceasefire deadline, pushing precious metals to fresh session highs.

headline: BONANZA GRADES: West Red Lake Hits 215 g/t Gold Over 5m at Madsen Mine
snippet: West Red Lake Gold (TSXV: WRLG) drilled 215.46 g/t Au over 5.35m — including 388.90 g/t over 2.95m — at the Austin 904 Complex in Ontario's Red Lake District, placing it among the highest-grade TSX-V junior drill results of 2026, with two rigs continuing in the 904 zone through year-end.

headline: EAGLE NUCLEAR Launches 27,000-ft Drill at America's Largest Uranium Deposit
snippet: Eagle Nuclear Energy (NASDAQ: NUCL) filed permit applications and engaged a drill contractor for a 47-hole, 27,000-ft program at its Aurora deposit in Oregon-Nevada — the largest conventional U.S. uranium resource at 32.75Mlbs indicated — targeting a pre-feasibility study by H2 2027, backed by $31.3M cash and no debt.

headline: SILVER NEAR $79: Metal Holds One-Month High as Supply Deficit Thesis Intact
snippet: Silver traded at $79.3/oz Wednesday, near a one-month high, as US-Iran ceasefire progress eased inflation fears — with structural support from the Silver Institute's projected 67-million-ounce supply deficit in 2026, the metal's sixth straight annual shortfall.

headline: COPPER RECOVERS to $6.10/lb, Two-Month High as Hormuz Anxiety Cools
snippet: Copper futures climbed toward $6.10/lb Wednesday, fully recovering losses from the onset of the Middle East conflict, as Washington-Tehran diplomacy revived Strait of Hormuz optimism and Chile's new government moved to streamline permitting and accelerate production.

headline: SILVER TARGETING $100? Asian Metals Analyst Sees Inventory Supercycle Igniting
snippet: GoldSeek's Asian Metals analyst argues that just-in-time inventory management is structurally finished and global corporate re-stocking will power a sustained non-ferrous metals rally, with silver needing seven consecutive daily closes above $76.20 before targeting $100/oz.

headline: K92 MINING Posts 46,743 oz AuEq in Q1, Ore Throughput Up 37% Year on Year
snippet: K92 Mining (TSX: KNT) processed 142,017 tonnes at Kainantu, Papua New Guinea in Q1 2026 — up 37% year-on-year — producing 46,743 oz AuEq with 95.1% gold recovery, setting a quarterly development record of 3,007m and maintaining full-year guidance of 190,000–225,000 oz.

headline: ABRASILVER Hits 171-Metre Gold Zone at Diablillos as Phase VI Drilling Begins
snippet: AbraSilver (TSX: ABRA) opened its fully-funded 15,000m Phase VI campaign at Diablillos, Argentina, with a lead result of 171m grading 0.83 g/t Au and 10.3 g/t Ag from 230m depth at Oculto East, as the company advances a Definitive Feasibility Study targeted for Q2 2026.

headline: CME LITHIUM FUTURES Break Monthly Volume Record as Prices Climb to CNY 167,500/T
snippet: CME Group's lithium carbonate futures hit 3,473 lots through April 10 with 14 days still remaining in the month, setting a new monthly volume record as Chinese spot prices rose to CNY 167,500/T — up more than 40% year-to-date — driven by EV policy and Beijing's plan to double national charging capacity by 2027.

headline: MP MATERIALS: Three Reasons the Rare Earth Stock Is Still Undervalued
snippet: With deepening U.S. government equity support, NdFeB magnet production ramping at its Fort Worth facility, and a consensus 12-month price target implying 36% upside, Motley Fool's analysis argues MP Materials (NYSE: MP) remains undervalued for investors building rare earth supply chain exposure.

headline: GOLD AT $4,800 Is No Fluke — Analysts See Fiscal and Monetary Crisis Signal
snippet: This analysis argues gold's April surge above $4,800 reflects structural anxiety about debt monetization and central bank credibility rather than geopolitical risk alone — and that any dip as Middle East tensions ease represents a buying opportunity on the primary long-term trend.

headline: GUYANA GOLD HUB: G Mining's C$3B G2 Goldfields Deal Reshapes Junior Sector
snippet: G Mining Ventures' (TSX: GMIN) C$3B acquisition of G2 Goldfields merges two adjacent Guyana projects into a potential 500koz+ annual gold producer, unlocking C$1B-plus in shared-infrastructure synergies — the Gold SWOT analysis calls it the most strategically significant junior M&A announcement of 2026.
